title: Hicriopteris bullata
intro: Hicriopteris bullata, also known as Turkish angelica, is a type of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, characterized by its distinctive fern-like leaves and tall, umbrella-shaped inflorescence. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Hicriopteris bullata include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Hicriopteris bullata is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of tinctures, salves, and ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Hicriopteris bullata is classified under the family Dryopteridaceae, commonly known as the wood fern family. This family is a part of the larger group known as Polypodiopsida, which includes all types of ferns. Within the class Polypodiopsida, Hicriopteris bullata falls under the order Polypodiales. The order Polypodiales is further divided into several families, with Dryopteridaceae being one of them. Hicriopteris bullata is also known as the southern maidenhair fern.
uses: Hicriopteris bullata is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia. However, there is limited information available on its medicinal uses.
